combat_history.event: Aquitaine provided maritime security and intelligence gathering in support of coalition operations against ISIS, operating in the eastern Mediterranean and Red Sea approaches.
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment demonstrated the class's intelligence gathering and network-centric warfare capabilities

combat_history.event: Provence conducted counter-piracy operations off Somalia, intercepting several suspicious vessels and providing helicopter support for merchant vessel escorts.
combat_history.significance: Validated the class's expeditionary capabilities and helicopter operations in high-threat environments

combat_history.event: Aquitaine launched SCALP Naval cruise missiles against Syrian chemical weapons facilities as part of coordinated strikes with US and UK forces.
combat_history.significance: First combat use of SCALP Naval from FREMM platform, demonstrating precision strike capability

combat_history.event: Multiple Aquitaine-class vessels participated in NATO maritime security operations in the Mediterranean, providing surveillance and interdiction capabilities.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated interoperability with NATO systems and sustained deployment capabilities

modernization.description: Comprehensive upgrade including enhanced electronic warfare systems, improved SETIS combat management system, and integration of new-generation sonars. Focus on cyber warfare resilience and network connectivity.
modernization.impact: Significantly enhances multi-domain warfare capability and extends operational relevance through 2040

modernization.description: Integration of SCALP Naval cruise missiles into SYLVER VLS system, providing land-attack capability up to 1000km range with GPS/terrain-following guidance.
modernization.impact: Transforms frigates into precision strike platforms capable of strategic targets

modernization.description: Integration with European FCAS network for cooperative engagement and shared sensor data, including manned-unmanned teaming capabilities.
modernization.impact: Will enable distributed lethality concepts and multi-platform targeting

vulnerabilities.description: Only 16 VLS cells compared to 32-48 on peer platforms limits sustained air defense capability and magazine depth for extended operations.
vulnerabilities.context: In high-intensity conflict against peer adversaries, missile inventory would be rapidly depleted without frequent replenishment
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Modular mission bay allows for additional missile storage, but requires manual reload capabilities

vulnerabilities.description: Older-generation EW systems are vulnerable to modern jamming and cyber attacks, particularly in GPS-denied environments.
vulnerabilities.context: Russian and Chinese EW capabilities could significantly degrade sensor and weapon effectiveness
vulnerabilities.mitigation: FREMM Evolution program addresses some EW gaps, but fundamental architecture limitations remain

vulnerabilities.description: Reliance on single MT30 gas turbine creates vulnerability if main propulsion is damaged, with limited redundancy from diesel generators.
vulnerabilities.context: Combat damage to main turbine could strand vessel or require lengthy repairs
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Diesel generators provide emergency propulsion but at significantly reduced speed and maneuverability

vulnerabilities.description: Minimal manning of 108 personnel creates challenges for damage control and sustained operations under combat conditions.
vulnerabilities.context: Modern naval combat requires extensive damage control teams and watch rotation for extended operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Automation systems compensate partially, but fundamental manning limitations constrain operational tempo

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Independent patrol, carrier task force escort, or NATO task group integration
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Often operates independently or with 1-2 support vessels, occasionally integrated with carrier groups
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo has strained maintenance schedules; typically 2-3 vessels available for immediate deployment

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 26 prioritizes ASW with quieter design and larger sonar suite, but Aquitaine offers better balanced multi-mission capability with land-attack missiles. Type 26 has larger crew but higher operating costs.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: ASW specialist vs. multi-mission generalist - different approaches to modern frigate design

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FFG-62 based on FREMM design but with Aegis systems and larger VLS magazine (32 cells). US version sacrifices some ASW capability for enhanced air defense and network integration with US Navy systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: How America adapted European frigate design for Pacific operations and Aegis integration

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Essentially same FREMM hull with Italian systems integration. Italian version emphasizes general purpose mission flexibility over specialized ASW capability, with different radar and combat management systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Tale of two FREMMs - how France and Italy customized the same design for different naval doctrines

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A has larger VLS magazine (32 cells) and longer range but lacks the ASW sophistication and stealth features. Chinese design prioritizes quantity production over individual platform capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Quality vs. quantity - sophisticated European frigate design versus mass-produced Chinese equivalent

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Gorshkov class carries larger anti-ship missiles (Kalibr/Oniks) and more VLS cells but has questionable reliability and limited production numbers. Russian design emphasizes firepower over sensors and sustainability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Western finesse versus Russian firepower - contrasting approaches to modern surface warfare
